interactions.label: Toggle Homography
interactions.teaches: This toggle applies a homography transformation to the grid, visibly skewing the perspective.

interactions.label: Arrow Orientation
interactions.teaches: Adjusting the slider changes the orientation of the arrow, reflecting the change in direction due to the transformation.

demo_steps.title: Initial Layout
demo_steps.narration: Let's begin with the original planar grid. The grid appears uniform, with right angles and equal spacing between lines. The arrow also points directly to the right.

demo_steps.title: Activating Homography
demo_steps.narration: When we toggle the homography, notice the grid's transformation. It now appears skewed, with angles no longer being right angles. This mimics the effect of changing perspective.

demo_steps.title: Adjusting Perspective Arrow
demo_steps.narration: Now, we adjust the arrow to reflect the perceived shift in direction. As the grid distorts, the arrow shows the direction of transformation, helping to visualize the homography's effect on orientation.

demo_steps.title: Preservation of Collinearity
demo_steps.narration: Despite these changes, note how lines on the grid remain straight. This demonstrates an important property of homographies: they preserve collinearity even when angles and lengths get distorted.

plan.camera_suggestion: A direct overhead view showing both grids side-by-side, with enough zoom to clearly see the transformation effect.
plan.notes: Arrow orientation should change direction smoothly to accurately reflect the transformation effects visually.

plan.contextChunk:
can use the equation of a line in homogeneous coordinates,
$\boldsymbol{l}^\mathsf{T}\mathbf{p}=0$. From equation
(@eq-homography_generic), we have that
$\mathbf{p} = \mathbf{H}^{-1} \mathbf{p}'$. Replacing the last equality
into the equation of the line gives
$(\mathbf{H} ^{-\mathsf{T}}\boldsymbol{l})^\mathsf{T}\mathbf{p'}=0$,
which corresponds to the equation of the line for the projected points
$\mathbf{p}'$.

In a general homography, angles and lengths are not preserved.
Therefore, parallel lines might not remain parallel, and lines of
identical length might have different lengths after a homography.
@fig-illustration_homography2 shows how a planar grid gets transformed
after applying a homography.

![Homography. Colinear points remain colinear after transformation. Angles between lines are not preserved.](figures/homography/example_homography.png){width="70%" #fig-illustration_homography2}

Let's start discussing in which scenarios the coordinates of points
between to images are related by a homography.

### Camera Rotation

Consider the following three images shown in @fig-example_three_images.

:::{#fig-example_three_images layout-ncol="3"}
![](figures/homography/3_IMG_7883_small.jpg){width="52%"}

![](figures/homography/2_IMG_7884_small.jpg){width="30%"}

![](figures/homography/1_IMG_7885_small.jpg){width="30%"}

interactions.label: Apply Homography
interactions.teaches: Turning this on applies a homography transformation to the grid, altering its appearance.

demo_steps.title: Start with Planar Grid
demo_steps.narration: Here we see a simple planar grid. Notice how all the angles are right angles and the grid squares are uniform.

demo_steps.title: Apply Homography
demo_steps.narration: Now, we toggle on the homography transformation. Observe how the grid distorts: angles are no longer right angles, and squares become trapezoids.

demo_steps.title: Understanding Distortion
demo_steps.narration: This distortion indicates that angles and lengths aren't preserved. However, note how lines remain straight, preserving collinearity under the homography.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: A front-facing view that captures both the original and transformed grids side by side, with moderate zoom.
interactionPlan.notes: Ensure smooth transition animations to illustrate the grid transformation clearly.

discrepancies.description: The generated image has two flat grids instead of one being skewed as in the reference.
The arrow in the generated image does not change its transformation as in the reference.

evaluation.notes: Missing 3D perspective on the right grid and lack of effective interaction for transformation understanding.
action_items: Implement the skew transformation on the right grid to reflect perspective correctly.
Add interactive features that allow the transformation to be applied visibly to the grids.
Ensure the arrow changes orientation to reflect the transformation effect accurately.

feedback.rationale: The need for a skewed grid and interactive transformations indicates a misunderstanding of the reference model and required interactions, suggesting the plan is flawed.
